free_text: 6Li+15N interaction at Ec.m.=23.1 MeV: Validation of
the alpha+d cluster model of 6Li
Cyclotron U-200P
A self-supporting 0.8-mg/cm2 lithium foil enriched in
6Li to about 85% was used as the target.
Four dE-E telescopes with40-mu-m-thick
silicon dE detectors and 0.3-mm-thick silicon E
detectors.
Corrected for background.
Spectroscopic amplitudes, parameters of the
nucleus-nucleus potentials were determined. Optical
model and the coupled-reaction-channels method were
used.
Errors include both the statistical errors
and uncertainties from the spectrum fitting procedure.
The errors in extraction of the peak
areas were estimated to be about 20% if the peaks were
well resolved and 30-40% for poorly resolved peaks.
The normalization error.
The relative angular distribution of the 15N+6Li
elastic scattering was normalized to calculated
optical model cross sections at small angles. This
factor was then used to convert all the relative
angular distribution data into absolute cross sections.
